steps.title: Identify the angle configuration from parallel lines
steps.content: When a transversal crosses two parallel lines, it creates 8 angles total. The problem states there are 4 acute and 4 obtuse angles, meaning the angles form two groups: 4 congruent acute angles and 4 congruent obtuse angles. Adjacent angles at each intersection are supplementary, so if the acute angles each measure $\alpha$, the obtuse angles each measure $180° - \alpha$. Since one of these 8 angles is $(7x - 250)°$, the two measures must be $(7x - 250)°$ and its supplement $180° - (7x - 250)° = (430 - 7x)°$.

steps.title: Determine all possible sums of 4 angles
steps.content:
The 8 angles consist of exactly 4 copies of $(7x - 250)°$ and 4 copies of $(430 - 7x)°$. Any sum of 4 angles selected from these 8 takes the form $a(7x - 250) + (4 - a)(430 - 7x)$, where $a$ is the number of $(7x - 250)°$ angles selected, with $a \in \{0, 1, 2, 3, 4\}$.

Computing each case:
  • $a = 0$: $4(430 - 7x) = 1720 - 28x$
  • $a = 1$: $(7x - 250) + 3(430 - 7x) = 7x - 250 + 1290 - 21x = -14x + 1040$
  • $a = 2$: $2(7x - 250) + 2(430 - 7x) = 14x - 500 + 860 - 14x = 360$
  • $a = 3$: $3(7x - 250) + (430 - 7x) = 21x - 750 + 430 - 7x = 14x - 320$
  • $a = 4$: $4(7x - 250) = 28x - 1000$
The possible values of $k$ are: $28x - 1000$, $1720 - 28x$, $14x - 320$, $360$, and $-14x + 1040$.

steps.title: Compare answer choices to possible sums
steps.content:
Testing each choice against the five possible sums:
  • Choice A: $-14x + 1540$ — This differs from the $a = 1$ case by 500 (which gave $-14x + 1040$). It does not match any of the five possible sums.
  • Choice B: $14x - 320$ — Matches the $a = 3$ case. ✓
  • Choice C: $-28x + 1720 = 1720 - 28x$ — Matches the $a = 0$ case. ✓
  • Choice D: $360$ — Matches the $a = 2$ case. ✓
That matches choice A — A is correct.
